report_name: Sri Lanka's CERT Reports Alarming Surge in Phishing and Ransomware Attacks

threat_actors.name: Cybercriminals Targeting Sri Lanka
threat_actors.description: Unspecified cybercriminals responsible for a surge in phishing, ransomware, and Business Email Compromise (BEC) attacks against Sri Lanka, leveraging the country's rapid digitalization and low security awareness. Their sophistication is noted to be increasing with the use of Generative AI.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Identified as a primary threat in a surge of attacks targeting Sri Lanka, including for Business Email Compromise (BEC).

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Used in ransomware attacks targeting Sri Lankan organizations, posing a significant economic threat to the nation.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: A specific phishing technique noted in the pre-mapped MITRE ATT&CK techniques for the attacks targeting Sri Lanka.

tools.name: Generative AI
tools.description: Leveraged by cybercriminals to increase the sophistication and volume of phishing and other attacks targeting Sri Lanka.

identities.name: Sri Lanka Computer Emergency Readiness Team (CERT)
identities.description: The national CERT for Sri Lanka, which reported the surge in cyber attacks and is involved in incident response and issuing warnings.

identities.name: Sri Lankan Businesses and Government Agencies
identities.description: The primary targets of the phishing and ransomware surge in Sri Lanka, affected by the country's rapid digitalization in banking, e-commerce, and government services.

relationships.target_name: Sri Lankan Businesses and Government Agencies

relationships.source_name: Sri Lankan Businesses and Government Agencies
Sri Lanka Computer Emergency Readiness Team (CERT)
